Exmouth town-centre wheelchair access map

Conservative MCP-Geo route filter for a Quantum iLevel user. This comparison tests the station-to-town-centre-to-seafront core using width, slope, lighting, and pedestrian-path evidence. Hover a coloured route in HTML for details. Dropped kerbs and crossing quality are not.

Context
Simplified context is shown. Wheel to zoom, drag to pan, and load OS Light when a browser key is set.

Preferred route Cleared pavement or sealed path, gentle slope, better lighting.
Use with care Pinch point, moderate slope, or weaker lighting.
Barrier in data Steps, footbridge, subway, or steep path section.
Context only Shown for orientation. It did not clear the wheelchair route filter.

Check before travel

  • Grey streets are context only. They did not clear the wheelchair route filter.
  • Final approach still needs checking: Exmouth railway station about 22 m; Exmouth indoor market about 28 m.
  • Not captured in current data: dropped kerbs, crossing type, tactile paving, parked cars, bins, café furniture, or temporary works.
  • In HTML, hover a coloured route for evidence text, wheel to zoom, drag to pan, and use the OS Light toggle for sharper street-name context when a browser-stored key is available.
Live MCP-Geo extract on 2026-03-07: roads, paths, pavement polygons, rail detail, water context, and named anchors. Route filter uses UK accessible-footway guidance, a conservative 5% maximum grade rule, and optional zoom-aware OS Light browser tiles for map context.

Load OS Light basemap

Enter your OS Maps API key to request `Light_3857` tiles directly from Ordnance Survey. The key is stored only in this browser on this machine and is never written into the report file.
